title: Build enforceable Fable pilot routing, cap, evidence, expiry, and rollback

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Real implementation following the pilot-evaluation issue's PILOT LONGER conclusion; a prior doc-only attempt was closed unmerged after being found non-executable. This head follows a fix commit that dropped a persistent agent file after the File Scope Lock CI check correctly rejected a mid-lane scope expansion.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Not independent Griff review, t1-approved, or a pm-verdict/v1 owner artifact.

runtime_proof.note: Docs/governance/ops-tooling diff -- no runtime code path changed. Per this repo's precedent for doc-only/governance T1 lanes (UTV2-1503, UTV2-1568), the standalone live-DB smoke is still run for real (not waived) against Supabase.

queries: database repository bundle persists a submission and settlement when Supabase is configured
UTV2-920: invalid atomic enqueue writes no lifecycle event or outbox row
UTV2-920: invalid atomic delivery confirmation rolls back outbox status, receipt, lifecycle, and audit writes
UTV2-920: invalid atomic settlement writes no settlement, lifecycle event, or audit row
UTV2-883: no duplicate participants for the same external_id and sport
UTV2-996: re-settling a settled pick creates correction -- no true duplicate base rows
UTV2-996: correction chain is additive -- original settlement row is not mutated

known_gaps: docs/05_operations/LANE_MANIFEST_SPEC.md's planned Section 17 addition documenting planning_model_routing was dropped from this diff because a live, concurrently-running lane (UTV2-1571) held an active file-scope lease on that exact file at implementation time. The field is documented in the JSON schema and in three-brain.md/OPERATING_MODEL_SONNET5.md instead.

The pilot itself has not been activated and has not run a single real qualifying task -- the mechanism is proven correct by unit test, not live pilot usage, since activation is explicitly out of this lane's scope.

An earlier commit on this branch registered a persistent .claude/agents/fable-pilot-reviewer.md subagent and a corresponding docs/05_operations/system-alignment-registry.json entry; the File Scope Lock CI check correctly rejected the resulting mid-lane scope expansion (that check reads file_scope_lock from the lane's first commit only, by design). Fixed by dropping the persistent agent entirely -- the advisory Fable review is now an ad-hoc background Agent() call in dispatch.md Phase 5, needing no registry entry.
